reasoning: My analysis suggests Martinique's protest activity peaked in September-October 2024 with violent unrest over cost of living, followed by government crackdowns (bans, curfews, riot police deployment). By March 2025, protests had 'largely given way to a movement' that won concessions including promised legislation on grocery prices. The 7-month gap in protest news from April-October 2025 indicates stabilization. Since the baseline of 3.167 includes this 2024 peak, the threshold is elevated. Current evidence suggests protest activity has subsided below this elevated baseline, making it less likely to exceed. The probabilities increase slightly with forecast horizon due to greater uncertainty about whether underlying issues (40% higher food costs, water prices 11x mainland rates, 27% poverty) will resurface and trigger renewed protests, especially if concessions prove insufficient. The short-term (Nov 2) is lowest as immediate stability is most certain, while Jan 24 allows more time for tensions to rebuild.
